description.en: Return a new Path object with each of the other arguments joined. The following are equivalent: >>> Path(...).joinpath('child').joinpath('grandchild') >>> Path(...).joinpath('child', 'grandchild') >>> Path(...) / 'child' / 'grandchild' Changed in version 3.10: Prior to 3.10, joinpath was undocumented and accepted exactly one parameter.

excerpt.es: Return a new Path object with each of the other arguments joined. The following are equivalent: >>> Path(...).joinpath('child').joinpath('grandchild') >>> Path(...).joinpath('child', 'grandchild') >>>...
excerpt.en: Return a new Path object with each of the other arguments joined. The following are equivalent: >>> Path(...).joinpath('child').joinpath('grandchild') >>> Path(...).joinpath('child', 'grandchild') >>>...
